initiatives.name: BTC Webinar Partner Program
initiatives.goal: Build a series of co-branded webinars with organizations that have distribution to small-check investors, climate tech founders, and operators. Grow BTC visibility ahead of the sponsorship platform launch.
initiatives.core_question: Which partner organizations have the right distribution and credibility to co-host webinars that position BTC as the go-to climate tech nano VC?
initiatives.notes: Bryan sourced 5 partner orgs: SVX, OCTIA, Clean Tech North, CAFIID, Good Investor (Tim). Webinar content is a preview of the Climate Tech Exit Lab. CAFIID member activation (LinkedIn ABM) is a related initiative — run by Bryan Duarte before activating.
initiatives.trigger: Bryan identifies org as a fit — warm intro path

initiatives.name: BTC Institutional Investor ABM — Canada
initiatives.timeline: Q2-Q3 2026 — sponsorship ask by end of summer
initiatives.goal: Build warm relationships with Canadian impact foundations and institutional investors toward a sponsorship or LP ask by end of summer 2026.
initiatives.core_question: Which Canadian institutional investors are the most viable path to a first check or sponsorship conversation by September 2026?
initiatives.notes: Targets: Toronto Foundation, Atmospheric Fund, Boann, Equality Fund. Meeting with Equality Fund in April 2026. Equality Fund contact will intro an intermediary for impact foundation due diligence.
initiatives.trigger: Bryan identifies target — or intro path unlocked via Equality Fund intermediary

initiatives.name: BTC Compliance Calendar Optimization
initiatives.goal: BTC has an optimized, automated compliance calendar reducing manual tracking and compliance risk.
initiatives.core_question: What are the key compliance deadlines for climate tech fund operations?
initiatives.notes: Followed up with a TIP member for feedback. Awaiting their response.

initiatives.name: BTC Climate Tech Exit Lab
initiatives.goal: Build a comprehensive interview series and collective intelligence repository on climate tech exits.
initiatives.core_question: How do we systematize climate tech exit learnings for founders?
initiatives.notes: Foundational BTC initiative. Monthly interviews with experienced founders, advisory committee formation, sponsorship pipeline development. The Exit Lab is the content backbone for the webinar partner program.
initiatives.trigger: Ongoing — Bryan and Keyona identify interview candidates

initiatives.name: CAFIID Member Activation — LinkedIn ABM + Webinar Pipeline
initiatives.timeline: TBD — run by Bryan Duarte first
initiatives.goal: Activate individual CAFIID members as an ABM layer via LinkedIn once BTC has brand exposure through a CAFIID co-hosted webinar, or independently if the org-level pitch doesn't move fast enough.
initiatives.core_question: Which CAFIID members are the best individual targets, and does the org-level webinar collab need to land first or can member-level outreach run independently?
initiatives.notes: CAFIID members include SVX, impact foundations, fund managers, and development finance institutions. Theory: co-hosted webinar builds BTC brand credibility with the CAFIID community, then individual member LinkedIn outreach lands warmer. Run the approach by Bryan Duarte before activating.
initiatives.trigger: CAFIID org-level webinar confirmed — or Bryan decides to run member-level outreach independently

initiatives.name: Work on Climate Slack — Event Poster Activation
initiatives.goal: Identify and qualify the most relevant people posting events in the Work on Climate Slack group and other climate Slack communities, then convert the highest-fit contacts into webinar partners, Exit Lab participants, or warm BTC relationships.
initiatives.core_question: Who is actively doing climate work and publicly signaling it through event posts, and which of them are the right fit for a webinar collab or Exit Lab conversation?
initiatives.notes: Signal source: event posts in Work on Climate Slack and similar climate Slack groups. People who post events are self-selecting as active, public-facing, and already doing real work — this is a warm signal without cold outreach friction. Process: (1) Scrape or manually capture event post data from relevant Slack channels. (2) Do lightweight research on each poster — who they are, what org they represent, what the event is about. (3) Score for fit against two use cases: webinar co-host for BTC or guest/participant for the Climate Tech Exit Lab. (4) Draft personalized outreach referencing their specific event or work. Outreach tone should feel peer-to-peer, not transactional. This is a sourcing initiative — campaigns get created per contact once research is done.
initiatives.trigger: Contact posts an event in a climate Slack channel

initiatives.name: Top Tier Impacts — Member Audit (WhatsApp + Email)
initiatives.goal: Audit the Top Tier Impacts WhatsApp group and email list to identify which members are the best fit for BTC's comms and marketing initiatives (webinar partners, Exit Lab guests) and which are viable deal flow or investing pipeline candidates.
initiatives.core_question: Which Top Tier Impacts members should go into the BTC comms pipeline, and which should go into the investing pipeline — and do any qualify for both?
initiatives.notes: Keyona is in the Top Tier Impacts WhatsApp group and email list. Access to the full member list needs to be confirmed — it's unclear whether full contact details are still accessible. First step is to pull whatever member list is available (WhatsApp contacts, email recipients) and do lightweight research on each person. Score each member against two tracks: (1) Comms fit — are they a good webinar co-host, Exit Lab guest, or warm BTC relationship to develop? (2) Investing fit — are they a founder or operator in climate tech that could be a deal flow candidate or portfolio company? Members who qualify for both tracks get flagged separately. This is a sourcing and qualification initiative — outreach and campaigns come after the audit is done.
initiatives.trigger: Keyona reviews WhatsApp group or email list and begins member audit
